text:
Shedule (シェジュール, Shejūru) is a member of Prince Halkenburg's private army. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 362 

Appearance

Shedule is a bespectacled young man with short fair hair, a broad forehead, and freckles. As a Royal Bodyguard, he wears a black suit and a tie. 

Personality

Shedule, like the rest of the bodyguards under Prince Halkenburg's service, seems to share the same will as their employer. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 372 He looked at his mark with fondness. He appears to be more emotional than Yuhirai. 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

On the first day of the voyage, 9 th Prince Halkenburg retires from the departure ceremony back to Room 1009 where he finds all his guards, including Shedule, lying on the floor unconscious before collapsing himself right after. He awakens several hours later in Room 1009's master bedroom and barges out, only to see his bodyguards all unpacking things and organizing his room as if nothing had happened, and wonders if what he saw was a dream. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 366 

Later that night, Halkenburg is contacted by Room 1014 and agrees to send his bodyguards to learn Nen under Kurapika. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 368 Shedule and Yuhirai are sent to learn Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 369 

At 9 a.m. the next morning, Shedule and Yuhirai assemble at Room 1014 for Kurapika's Nen lessons. As Shedule looks at the mark of a pinion on the back of his left hand and guesses that it was the work of Halkenburg's Nen beast, he thinks back to Halkenburg's instructions for attending. Halkenburg had suggested their common mark must have something to do with the Nen beasts that 14 th Prince Woble's guard had called about, and told them to find out what it was, allowing them to disclose whatever they need to learn all they can. Yuhirai agrees and says that it is the mark of resolve that Halkenburg always espoused. 

Shedule later witnesses the death of Barrigen at the hands of four snake-like beasts. Yuhirai surmises that the snake attack was a ploy to disrupt the Nen training and Shedule believes that it was an attack by a Prince who didn't send a representative. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 370 Following the murder, Kurapika tells his students to decide whether to stay or leave. Yuhirai and Shedule discuss it over and decide to remain, so Kurapika teaches them his first exercise. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 371 Kurapika takes notice of his and Yuhirai's aura output and enquires if they are testing him. Shocked, Shedule frantically rejects the accusation and Yuhirai asks to speak with Kurapika about it. Kurapika agrees to speak with one of them about it after lessons that day. 

On the third day, Halkenburg has his personal guards gather for a moment of silence for 12 th Prince Momoze after her death the night before. Unbeknownst to them, their unison generates an enormous amount of aura observed by Shikaku, guard of 1 st Prince Benjamin, watching over them. Shikaku watches Halkenburg's Guardian Spirit Beast perched on his shoulders and reports the matter to Benjamin. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 375 

At 10:05 a.m. on the third day of the voyage, 4 th Prince Tserriednich's guard Myuhan is killed during Nen lessons in Room 1014, and his corpse is found in a similar state to Barrigen's. Maor, Captain of 5 th Prince Tubeppa's Guard and sent by her to attend Nen lessons, and Satobi, Captain of 2 nd Queen Duazul's Guard, assigned to guard 7 th Prince Luzurus and sent by him to attend Nen lessons, accuse Kurapika of luring the men of enemies of Woble under the promise of learning Nen to eliminate them one by one. Belerainte, Hunter and guard of 13 th Prince Marayam defends Kurapika and blames Myuhan for acting carelessly and using the bathroom while in hostile territory. Kurapika calls Belerainte over to thank him privately, though the act is watched with suspicion by the other participants of the class, including Shedule. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 376 

On the seventh day of the voyage, Shedule is still taking part in Kurapika's Nen lessons. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 382 Like everyone else, he begins to have doubts about the training, since they always repeat the same exercise. The following day, he is amazed by Kurapika's Water Divination, wondering whether it counts as a special power. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 386 Shedule waits in the hall until the end of the session while Ladiolus, Maor, Yuri and Satobi are called one by one to the main bedroom supposedly to perform Water Divination. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 388 On the tenth of the voyage, Tenftory reports to Prince Zhang Lei that all of the students who have taken Kurapika's Nen training, are able to utilize Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 389 

On the tenth day, Shedule turns up at Room 1014 for Kurapika's Nen lessons. Kurapika and Bill invite Longhi into Room 1014's master bedroom to have her Nen divined, but takes an unnaturally long amount of time, prompting Sakata to knock and ask if anything is wrong. Kurapika steps out and says that negotiations are taking a while because they and Longhi are unable to agree on the terms of the truce, but promises to clear up any doubts the participants have later. Kurapika steps back in, and a while later returns to Room 1014's living room together with Longhi. Kurapika apologizes and explains that they are still unable to agree on the details, and invites Tenftory in to have his Nen divined. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 401 

Around noon on the twelfth day of the voyage, twelve of Halkenburg's guards, including Shedule, are seen outside the general hospital as Halkenburg's pallbearers, to carry his casket from Tier 3 to Tier 1. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 406 Shedule and Halkenburg's other guards carry Halkenburg's casket up the staircase from Tier 3 to Tier 2. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 407 

Abilities & Powers

Nen

Due to the power of Prince Halkenburg's Guardian Spirit Beast, Shedule has reached a state of half-awakening in which he is unable to see aura, but he has a superior output compared to someone who only just opened their aura nodes. However, Kurapika speculated that by receiving the Nen beast's mark Shedule was made to share its Limitations and penalties, which might be as severe as the loss of one's life if the conditions of the ability are breached. 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

For the purposes of Kurapika's Nen training, Shedule is a "prayer".
